content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 25, 2000

Sandi Genser-Maack
North & East Neighborhood Council
Post Office Box 254
Richmond, CA 94808

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. G-00-108

Dear Ms. Genser-Maack:

This letter responds to your May 8, 2000, request for advice under the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹.

As I explained to Julie Freestone of your office in our telephone conversation of May 23, 2000, the Act requires the Commission to provide formal written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b).) The Commission does not provide advice to third parties regarding a public official's duties under the Act. (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(b)(8).) Consequently, we are unable to provide assistance with respect to the issues you raise in your letter. However, we have enclosed the following materials for your information: 1) Fact Sheet — Mass Mailings Under the Political Reform Act; 2) Regulation 18901 — Mass Mailings Sent at Public Expense; 3) Gartner Advice Letter, No. A-99-224; and 4) Martyn Advice Letter, No. A-99-103.

We are aware of various concerns regarding the mass mailing provisions of the Act; there will be a discussion at the June 2, 2000, Commission Meeting regarding some of these concerns. I am enclosing a copy of the agenda for the June meeting, along with a copy of the memorandum prepared by Legal Division staff outlining two specific requests to amend Regulation 18901 — Mass Mailings Sent at Public Expense. If you are unable to attend the meeting, feel free to submit your comments in writing by 5:30 p.m. on Wednesday, May 31, 2000 (comments may be submitted by hand delivery, mail or fax). You have been added to the Commission's mailing list to receive ongoing information regarding the mass mailing regulation.

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

File No. 00-108
Page 2

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Lynda Doherty
Political Reform Consultant

LD:tls
Enclosures

cc: Julie Freestone, Committee Chair

I:\Advice Ltrs\00108.wpd

sections.conclusion: The Commission does not provide advice to third parties regarding a public official's duties under the Act. (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(b)(8).) Consequently, the Commission is unable to provide assistance with respect to the issues raised.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought FPPC advice regarding mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act affecting a public official.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requestor is a third party rather than the public official whose duties are in question. The Commission is limited to providing formal written advice only to the person whose duties are in question or their authorized representative.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter. No traditional QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; instead, the legal issue and reasoning are embedded in the body of the letter. The document clearly states the reason for declination under Section 83114(b) and Regulation 18329(b)(8).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester sought FPPC advice regarding mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act affecting a public official.

CONCLUSION: The Commission does not provide advice to third parties regarding a public official's duties under the Act. (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(b)(8).) Consequently, the Commission is unable to provide assistance with respect to the issues raised.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined a request for advice from the North & East Neighborhood Council regarding mass mailing provisions, explaining that it can only provide advice to the public official in question or their representative, not to third parties. The letter directed the requestor to relevant materials and invited submission of written comments for a scheduled Commission meeting on mass mailing regulations.
